title: Glorious Things of Thee Are Spoken
author: John Newton
story: Newton, the former slave trader who wrote Amazing Grace, composed this celebration of Zion as God's dwelling place. Haydn's Austrian Hymn melody lends it a grandeur befitting the New Jerusalem.

lines: Glorious things of thee are spoken, Zion city of our God
He, Whose Word cannot be broken Formed thee for His Own abode: On the Rock of Ages founded, What can shake thy sure repose
With salvation's walls surrounded, Thou mayst smile at all thy foes
See, the streams of living waters, Springing from Eternal Love, Well supply thy sons and daughters, And all fear of want remove: Who can faint, while such a river Ever will their thirst assuage
Grace, which, like the Lord the Giver, Never fails from age to age
Round each habitation hov'ring, See the cloud and fire appear, For a glory and a cov'ring: Showing that the Lord is near
Thus deriving from their banner Light by night, and shade by day, Safe they feed upon the manna, Which He gives them on their way
Savior, if of Zion's city I
Let the world deride or pity, I will glory in Thy Name: Fading is the worldling's pleasure
All his boasted pomp and show: Solid joys and lasting treasure None but Zion's children know.
